title: 控方证人
summary:
Seleras Hammel experiences recurring, transcendental visions characterized by a joyful, ascending melody that induces a sensation of soaring flight. These episodes are followed by a violent, heavy pulling force that drags him back to reality, causing intense physical pain in his limbs and ears along with a suffocating sense of oppression. To cope with the feeling of being trapped by walls and ceilings, Hammel has moved to a sparsely furnished room at the top of his house to face the sky, though he still feels a longing for the open countryside.

During a private dinner, Hammel confesses his fears of insanity to his friend Bernard Seldon, a psychiatrist. Hammel explains that these phenomena began on a winter night following a dinner with Seldon. He describes his visions as evolving, noting that he is developing non-physical senses that allow him to perceive light, color, and sound beyond human capability. He specifically mentions sensing the colors of magnificent wings, though he cannot describe them using earthly terms. Hammel describes an agonizing internal struggle between these "wings," representing spiritual or transcendent states, and his worldly attachments to luxury and pleasure, which pull him in the opposite direction.

Seldon suggests rational explanations, such as hypnosis, nervous instability, or dreams, but acknowledges that some aspects remain inexplicable. He advises Hammel to either move to the countryside or seek out the legless street performer whom Hammel believes possesses supernatural powers. Hammel refuses both suggestions, stating he cannot afford to lose his luxuries and is afraid of the man. Seldon concludes that Hammel must wait to see what happens next, while Hammel remains desperate regarding his internal battle.
